vaultHealth.title: Vault Health
vaultHealth.subtitle: A quick snapshot of your vault before you dive in.

hero.title: Your vault is indexed. Pick one useful next step.
hero.body: Nexusky keeps the files local, then uses the index to explain what is worth asking, repairing, or mapping next.

scanning.title: Building a local snapshot
scanning.desc: Reading files, links, tasks, and memory signals without changing your notes.

error.title: Vault health is unavailable
error.desc: You can still open the vault and keep working. Details:

askAi.title: Ask AI about your vault
askAi.desc: Start a vault-wide question and cite the notes used.
askAi.prompt: Give me a sourced tour of this vault. Use local search and the Context Pack, cite the notes you use, and say clearly if no relevant notes are found. Current health: {{notes}} notes, {{links}} links, {{unresolved}} unresolved links, {{orphans}} isolated notes, {{tasks}} open tasks, {{missingMemory}} notes without Memory Ledger.

fixLinks.title: Resolve {{count}} unresolved links
fixLinks.desc: Open a Chat draft that lists broken wikilink sources and repair suggestions.
fixLinks.prompt: Help me review the {{unresolved}} unresolved wikilinks in this vault. Use local search and the Context Pack, group broken links by source note, suggest likely targets with confidence, and do not write files directly. If a change is useful, explain how it should go through preview or a reversible workflow. Current health: {{notes}} notes, {{links}} links, {{orphans}} isolated notes.

reviewStructure.title: Review {{count}} structure signals
reviewStructure.desc: Isolated notes open in graph focus; duplicate titles get a Chat review.
reviewStructure.prompt: Review this vault's structure issues: {{orphans}} isolated notes and {{duplicates}} duplicate-title groups. Explain which issues most affect retrieval, graph clarity, and source trust, then propose previewable fixes. Do not write files directly.

reviewTasks.title: Review {{count}} open tasks
reviewTasks.desc: Open a Chat draft that groups open tasks by source note.
reviewTasks.prompt: Review the {{tasks}} open Markdown tasks in this vault. Group them by source note, recommend which should stay open, be completed, deferred, or archived, cite the notes you used, and do not modify files directly.

reviewMemory.title: Review {{count}} notes without Memory Ledger
reviewMemory.desc: Open a Chat draft that finds the notes most worth adding memory evidence for.
reviewMemory.prompt: Review the {{missingMemory}} notes without Memory Ledger in this vault. Prioritize the notes most worth adding explainable long-context evidence for, explain why, name related themes, and suggest next steps. Do not create or write memory files directly.

reviewStale.title: Review {{count}} stale notes
reviewStale.desc: Open a Chat draft that finds old notes needing updates, links, or archiving.
reviewStale.prompt: Review the {{stale}} notes untouched for more than 60 days. Use local search and the Context Pack to identify which stale notes most need updates, links, or archiving, explain why, and propose previewable next steps. Do not write files directly.

browseGraph.title: Browse the knowledge graph
browseGraph.desc: See clusters, bridges, and isolated notes at a glance.

welcome.subtitle: Local Markdown stays in your folder. Nexusky builds memory, search, and reviewable AI actions around it.

samples.label: Workflow samples
samples.description: Create a sample vault with real notes for research, writing, development, or learning.
samples.created: Created sample vault at {{path}} with {{files}} files

research.title: Research
research.description: Literature notes, source notes, digest writing, and weekly review.

writing.title: Writing
writing.description: Essay brief, source notes, draft fragments, and revision review.

developer.title: Developer
developer.description: ADRs, debug notes, API notes, and engineering review.

learning.title: Learning
learning.description: Lesson notes, concepts, practice drills, and weekly review.

graph.title: Graph
graph.emptyHint: Use [[links]] in your notes to build connections

orphans.prompt: Review this graph maintenance signal: isolated notes. Count: {{count}}. Example nodes: {{samples}}. Use local search and the Context Pack to explain why these notes may be isolated, suggest link/tag/structure fixes, cite sources when available, and do not write files directly. If a fix is useful, describe a preview-first or reversible path.

bridges.prompt: Review this graph maintenance signal: cross-folder bridge paths. Count: {{count}}. Example edges: {{samples}}. Explain which bridges look useful, noisy, or missing stronger authored links. Use local search and the Context Pack, cite sources when available, and do not write files directly. If a fix is useful, describe a preview-first or reversible path.

inferred.prompt: Review this graph maintenance signal: AI-inferred relations. Count: {{count}}. Example inferred edges: {{samples}}. Explain which inferred relations deserve confirmation, authored links, tags, or dismissal. Use local search and the Context Pack, cite sources when available, and do not write files directly. If a fix is useful, describe a preview-first or reversible path.

quickActions.title: Next actions
quickActions.subtitle: The top three actionable signals from Vault Health.
quickActions.boundary: Opens a route, never writes automatically
quickActions.empty: No priority health actions need review right now.

aiWorkspace.noProviderToast: Configure an AI provider to use chat, edit, or Vault tools. Files, Search, Graph, Vault Health, and Maintenance still work without AI.

aiProviderErrors.empty: AI request failed. Try again or check provider settings.
aiProviderErrors.localArchitecture: The app architecture does not match this machine. Install the build for your processor.

kinds.api_key: API key or permission problem. Check the key, account access, and billing status.
kinds.model: Model name problem. Choose a model supported by this provider.
kinds.network: Network or Base URL problem. Check the endpoint, proxy, DNS, and local service status.
kinds.rate_limit: Rate limit or quota problem. Wait, reduce request volume, or check quota and billing.
kinds.context: Context is too long for the selected model. Use a larger-context model or send less context.
kinds.timeout: Provider request timed out. Retry or switch to a faster endpoint.
kinds.unknown: AI request failed. Check provider settings and try again.

chatMessages.emptyHint: You can also type a question, or use @ to cite notes.

vaultToolsBoundary.title: Vault tools available: {{readOnly}} read-only tools, {{previewWrite}} preview-write tools, {{agentOnly}} execution-only tools. Writes still require edit mode, maintenance preview, or reviewable execution.

noVaultEvidence.title: No local source found

publishScope.title: Publish preview
publishScope.description: Pick the content to publish, preview navigation, links, and missing resources, then choose an output folder.

all.label: Entire vault
all.hint: Publish every Markdown note and asset.

folder.label: Folder
folder.hint: Publish one folder subtree and its local assets.

tag.label: Tag
tag.hint: Publish notes that carry the selected tag.

property.label: Property
property.hint: Publish notes that match a property key and optional value.

publishScope.previewEmpty: Choose a scope, then preview the published site before writing files.
publishScope.readyHint: Preview ready. Choose Publish to select an output folder.
publishScope.issueHint: Preview found missing links or resources. You can still publish after reviewing them.
publishScope.accessBoundary: Static export access still depends on the hosting provider. Public mode keeps the export indexable, private mode adds noindex and robots blocking for the generated site.
publishScope.unpublishConfirmMessage: This removes the generated publish files from {{path}} and clears the saved publish target.

public.label: Public
public.hint: Generated pages can be indexed unless the host applies stricter rules.

private.label: Private
private.hint: Generated pages include noindex and robots blocking.

bases.title: Properties View
bases.searchPlaceholder: Search titles, paths, tags, aliases, or any property...
bases.emptyHint: Add frontmatter at the top of a note, or use the right properties panel to fill title, tags, aliases, and other fields.
bases.guideTitle: How to use the properties view
bases.guideFrontmatter: 1. Add frontmatter to notes, or maintain title, aliases, tags, and cssclasses in the right properties panel.
bases.guideSearch: 2. Use search, tag filtering, and sorting to manage projects, tasks, courses, or article status.
bases.guideEdit: 3. Double-click a cell to edit a property; saving writes back to the original Markdown file.
bases.columnsHint: Choose which custom frontmatter fields are shown. Core columns are always visible.
bases.noCustomColumns: This vault has no extra custom property columns yet.

canvas.title: Properties and Timeline
canvas.associationAlreadyLinked: This link already exists in the note

associationReason.tag: These cards share tags, so the relationship is strong enough to capture as a topic link.
associationReason.source: These cards came from the same source and may belong to the same reading or import context.
associationReason.title: Their titles share terms, which may point to the same concept or question.

canvas.guideDrag: 1. Drag cards to arrange them; layout is saved per vault.
canvas.guideOpen: 2. Double-click a card to open its Markdown note.
canvas.guideLinks: 3. Solid arrows come from [[wikilink]] relations in notes.
canvas.guideSuggestions: 4. Faint dashed lines are automatic association hints from shared tags, sources, or title terms.
canvas.guideZoom: 5. Ctrl/Cmd + mouse wheel or trackpad pinch zooms around the pointer.
canvas.guidePan: 6. Drag an empty canvas area to pan the view.
canvas.guideCreate: 7. New nodes appear near the current view center and avoid existing nodes.
canvas.focusMenu: Focus this card and its links
canvas.guideFocus: 8. Right-click a card to show only it and its direct links; press Esc or the top button to exit focus.

timeline.title: Timeline
timeline.summary: {{count}} notes, {{today}} updated today, {{week}} updated in the last 7 days
timeline.searchPlaceholder: Search titles, paths, tags, or aliases...

reader.title: Imported Reading
reader.searchPlaceholder: Search titles, authors, sources, tags, or status...

triageReason.unread: This item has not been processed yet, so it belongs at the front of the reading flow.
triageReason.read: This item has already been read. The next useful step is connecting it to a project, topic, or durable note.
triageReason.tagged: Existing tags point to a topic, so it is ready to be organized in the properties view.
triageReason.source: A source URL is available, making this a good item to reopen later for verification or capture.
triageReason.later: No clear status is present yet, so it is parked without interrupting the current reading flow.
triageReason.archived: This item is archived and stays out of the default processing queue.

reader.notePlaceholder: Capture what this changes, connects to, or needs next...
reader.aiDigestFailed: Failed to prepare AI digestion prompt
reader.emptyImports: No imported Notion, Readwise, or Pocket content yet

common.semanticFailed: AI semantic analysis failed, showing existing relations only
common.pluginPanelNoContent: This plugin panel has no content to display.

theme.title: Theme
theme.description: Themes apply across the sidebar, editor, dialogs, and AI panels.

accent.title: Accent color
accent.description: Overrides links, buttons, selection states, and focus color in the current theme.

themePackages.title: Theme packages
themePackages.description: Loads .nexusky/themes/*.json from the current vault and applies global color variables.
themePackages.builtinHint: Only apply the selected theme and accent color above.
themePackages.noVault: Open a vault to use theme packages.
themePackages.empty: No theme packages yet. Add .json files to .nexusky/themes, then refresh.

snippets.title: CSS snippets
snippets.description: Loads .nexusky/snippets/*.css from the current vault and lets each snippet be toggled independently.
snippets.noVault: Open a vault to use CSS snippets.
snippets.empty: No CSS snippets yet. Add .css files to .nexusky/snippets, then refresh.

ai.title: AI Providers

ai.savedApiKeyPlaceholder: Saved. Leave blank to keep the existing key
ai.baseUrlRequired: Base URL is required for OpenAI-compatible providers

noAiMode.title: No AI provider configured
noAiMode.description: You can keep using the local vault. Add a provider only when you want AI chat, editing, Vault tools, or reviewable execution.

cloudSync.title: Cloud Sync
cloudSync.description: Sync your notes to the cloud
cloudSync.notConfiguredDesc: Cloud sync feature is not configured yet, choose a cloud storage provider to get started

icloud.pathHint: Leave empty to auto-detect iCloud Drive path

onedrive.clientIdHint: Create an app in Azure Portal and get the client ID

longContext.title: Memory Ledger
longContext.enabledHint: Automatically generate and maintain long-term memory for notes
longContext.autoGenerateHint: Automatically generate memory ledger after note changes
longContext.retentionDaysHint: How long to keep memory ledgers (days)
longContext.maxTokensHint: Maximum token limit per memory ledger

syncHealth.title: Sync health
syncHealth.description: Check the latest sync, failure reason, and queued transfers for the active cloud backend.
syncHealth.recoveryHint: Sync overwrites save the previous Markdown version to History. Files removed by sync are moved to Trash before deletion.
syncHealth.boundaryHint: Local vaults and bring-your-own sync stay free. Official managed sync / backup remains a future paid add-on.
syncHealth.syncConflict: Sync finished with ↑{{pushed}} ↓{{pulled}} and {{count}} conflicts to resolve.

conflicts.title: Conflict recovery
conflicts.description: Both copies changed in the merge window. Review the timestamps and hashes, then keep the version you want.
conflicts.explanation: Keep local pushes the current vault copy back to the backend. Pull remote replaces the local file with the remote copy.
conflicts.keepLocalHint: Writes the vault copy back to the backend.
conflicts.pullRemoteHint: Overwrites the local file with the remote version.

configIssues.name: Add a provider name.
configIssues.api_key: Add an API key or keep a saved key.

errorKinds.api_key: Check the API key and account permissions.
errorKinds.model: Check the model name and provider type.
errorKinds.network: Check the Base URL and network connection.
errorKinds.rate_limit: Check quota, billing, or rate limits.
errorKinds.context: Use a model with a larger context window.
errorKinds.timeout: The request timed out. Try again or use a faster endpoint.
errorKinds.unknown: Check provider settings and try the test again.

proactive.title: Proactive suggestions
proactive.description: Surface noteworthy hints while the app analyzes relations, long-term themes, and tasks in the background.
proactive.enabledHint: Let AI analyze your notes and provide proactive suggestions.
proactive.frequencyHint: Controls how often AI provides suggestions.
proactive.categoriesHint: Choose which types of suggestions you want to receive.
proactive.completionHint: Inline AI text completion while typing in the editor
